title: Fleet Review Findings
description: Structured reviewer output emitted by adversarial-review-and-fix and other review missions. Every finding MUST cite a verbatim quoted_line from the source file so the orchestrator can grep-verify the claim. Findings whose quote can't be found in the cited file are marked unverified and DOWNGRADED in the fleet-outcome — this is the corpus-grounded counter to reviewer hallucination. Lineage: GodModeSkill work-converge quoted-line check + xreview internal/schema/review.json (confidence, fix_alternatives, fix_strategy).

schema_version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes.

mission.description: Mission that emitted these findings (typically adversarial-review-and-fix).

review_id.description: Stable identifier for this review run (e.g. <branch>-<round>-<reviewer>). Used for archive paths and cross-references.

reviewer.description: Optional: the reviewer agent identity (vendor/model/role). Surfaces in fleet-outcome for cross-vendor diversity audit.

round.description: Review round number (1-based). Disagreement loops increment this.

decision.description: approve = ready to integrate; request_changes = blocking findings exist; partial = some findings addressed but others remain.

reasoning.description: One paragraph: why approve, why not, or what partial means.

allOf.$comment: ROOT_CAUSE_DEPTH discipline (engine.md): findings tagged category=root_cause_depth MUST name the other call paths the root cause can trigger. A root_cause_depth finding without cascade_impact is almost always a symptom-fix finding miscategorised — the schema rejects it the same way it rejects an unverified quoted_line. Source: SWE-Review HARD RULE.

id.description: Stable identifier within this review (e.g. F-001). Survives across rounds — the same finding keeps the same id when re-asserted.

file_path.description: Path relative to the repo root (forward slashes). The verifier resolves it against the repo root and rejects paths that escape it (absolute paths or ../ traversal are not honoured outside the repo).

line_number.description: Optional 1-based line number where quoted_line appears. When present, the verifier anchors the match to a ±2-line window around it (issue #98); when absent, the quote must be at least 12 normalized characters for the whole-file match to count.

quoted_line.description: EXACT verbatim line from the source file, character-for-character including whitespace. The orchestrator runs a whitespace-tolerant grep against the cited file; findings whose quoted_line is not found are marked verified=false and DOWNGRADED in the quorum (likely hallucination from over-eager review). If you cannot quote the exact line, omit the finding.

context_lines.description: Optional: how many surrounding lines were considered (informational, not verified).

fix_alternatives.description: 1-4 labeled fix proposals. Forces the reviewer to think laterally rather than locking onto one fix. xreview lineage: 2-3 alternatives is the sweet spot.

label.description: A, B, C, D — single uppercase letter.

effort.description: minimal = ≤10 line change, moderate = single-file refactor, large = cross-module.

recommended.description: At most one alternative should be recommended=true. Builder MAY pick a different one with justification.

confidence.description: Reviewer's confidence (0-100) that this finding is a real defect. Below 60: ask the reviewer to either gather more evidence or omit. Above 80: candidate for auto-apply. Below 50 with severity=low: candidate to drop entirely.

fix_strategy.description: auto = builder may apply the recommended fix without human gating; ask = human must approve. Default to ask for severity=critical and for changes touching public APIs.

cascade_impact.description: Optional: what other code paths / call sites are affected by this defect. Helps the builder decide if the fix needs to be broader. Required for findings with category=root_cause_depth.

trigger.description: Optional: input or code path that triggers the bug. Useful for repro and EVID.

verified.description: Set by verify-findings, NOT by the reviewer. true = quoted_line found in cited file; false = quote not found (likely hallucination); absent = not yet verified.

verify_reason.description: Set by verify-findings when verified=false. Human-readable reason ('file not found', 'quoted-line not found in cited file', etc.).

blind_fix_chain.description: Optional: pointer to this finding's blind-fix attestation. The verifier (scripts/verify_blind_fix.py, Layer 3 of the substrate) uses this to override the canonical .fleet/runs/<run_id>/reviewer-blind-fix-<id>.md location. Path is always run-archive-relative (absolute paths and `..` escapes are rejected). See skills/autonomous-fleet-core/references/blind-fix.md.

path.description: Run-archive-relative path to the blind-fix file. Empty string falls back to the canonical location.

reviewer_quote_sha.description: Optional: git SHA of the commit where the reviewer wrote the quoted_line. Used for temporal ordering across reviewer/fixer commits.

fixer_draft_sha.description: Optional: git SHA of the fixer's draft commit. MUST be earlier than the reviewer's findings commit for the chain to verify.

integration_sha.description: Optional: git SHA of the merged/integrated fix commit.
